dataset_description: Bengaluru Traffic Police (BTP) illegal parking violations, Jan–Apr 2024

peak_enforcement_hours.narrative: On average, 86.67% of violations per hotspot occur during the defined peak enforcement hours (10 PM – 6 AM). This pattern likely reflects night patrol scheduling rather than actual daytime congestion.

headline_insights: The top-ranked hotspot is 'BTP051 - Safina Plaza Junction' with an impact score of 81.37 and 15,449 violations.
168 of 2221 hotspots are confirmed BTP junctions already known to enforcement; 2053 were newly discovered.
35,587 vehicles have been cited more than once; the most chronic offender has been cited 55 times.
Street-grouped HDBSCAN was used to prevent road-network chaining — a spatial clustering failure mode where a continuous road fuses into one artificially large false cluster.

Impact scores combine violation frequency (30%), congestion-severity weighted violation type (30%), peak-hour concentration (20%), and average vehicle footprint weight (20%), with a confidence discount for low-volume hotspots.

methodology_notes.junction_first_strategy: 168 BTP named junctions used as ground truth; 'No Junction' records clustered separately
